title: Initialization, Indeterminate Values, and Undefined Behavior
description: An optimized release makes an uninitialized-state bug disappear in debug and reappear with a different compiler. Learners cumulatively produce defined-state initialization audit through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Initialize every read-reachable scalar, member, padding-sensitive operation, and error-path state before use.
Avoid evaluating indeterminate values and understand when unsigned-char inspection is and is not permitted.
Explain how undefined behavior removes semantic constraints and invalidates debug-build intuition.
